message:
feat: session restore engine — detect and recover from unclean shutdowns

Adds session_restore.rs, which detects unclean ft shutdowns on startup
and orchestrates recovery using the latest persisted checkpoint.

On `ft watch` startup, the module:
  1. Queries mux_sessions for shutdown_clean=0 candidates
  2. Loads the latest checkpoint topology + pane state
  3. Makes a RestoreDecision (auto-restore, prompt user, or skip)
  4. Delegates to LayoutRestorer for split topology reconstruction
  5. Returns a RestoreSummary with pane mapping + statistics

Data flow: Database → SessionCandidate → RestoreDecision →
           LayoutRestorer → RestoreSummary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"wa-41w","title":"Connection pool for DirectMuxClient — eliminate CLI subprocess spawning","description":"## Goal
Wire the existing Pool<C> (crates/wa-core/src/pool.rs) to DirectMuxClient (crates/wa-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs). Instead of spawning `wezterm cli` subprocesses for every operation (which creates 60+ stuck processes), reuse persistent Unix socket connections.

## Background & Motivation
The #1 operational problem with wa under agent swarm workloads is stuck CLI processes. Every wa operation (get pane text, list panes, send keys) currently spawns a `wezterm cli` subprocess, which:
1. Creates a new TCP/Unix socket connection to the mux server
2. Performs the PDU handshake (verify_codec_version, register_client)
3. Sends one request, gets one response
4. Exits

Under 50+ pane load with 200ms polling, this spawns ~250 processes/second. When the mux server is under load, these processes pile up waiting for lock contention on the Mux singleton, creating 60+ zombie processes that consume file descriptors and memory.

The Pool<C> in pool.rs already has everything we need: idle timeout eviction, semaphore-based concurrency limiting, health checks, and metrics. DirectMuxClient already speaks the raw mux protocol over Unix sockets. We just need to connect them.

## Technical Design

### DirectMuxClientPool
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs (new file)
use crate::pool::{Pool, PoolConfig, PoolItem};
use crate::vendored::mux_client::DirectMuxClient;

pub type DirectMuxClientPool = Pool<DirectMuxClient>;

impl PoolItem for DirectMuxClient {
    async fn health_check(&self) -> bool {
        // Send a lightweight ping (GetPaneList with empty filter)
        self.list_panes().await.is_ok()
    }
    
    async fn create() -> Result<Self> {
        let socket_path = find_mux_socket()?;
        let mut client = DirectMuxClient::connect(&socket_path).await?;
        client.verify_codec_version().await?;
        client.register_client().await?;
        Ok(client)
    }
}
```

### Integration with WeztermClient
```rust
// In crates/wa-core/src/wezterm.rs, replace run_cli() calls:
impl WeztermClient {
    // Before: spawns subprocess
    // pub async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>> {
    //     let output = self.run_cli(&["cli", "list", "--format", "json"]).await?;
    //     ...
    // }
    
    // After: uses pool
    pub async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>> {
        let conn = self.pool.get().await?;
        let panes = conn.list_panes().await?;
        Ok(panes)
    }
}
```

### Pool Configuration
```toml
# wa.toml
[mux_pool]
max_connections = 8          # Enough for concurrent captures
idle_timeout_seconds = 300   # Evict idle connections after 5 min
health_check_interval = 30   # Periodic health check
acquire_timeout_seconds = 10 # Max wait for a connection
```

### Existing Code References
- Pool<C>: crates/wa-core/src/pool.rs (554 lines) — has idle eviction, semaphore, metrics
- DirectMuxClient: crates/wa-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs (997 lines) — has connect, verify_codec_version, register_client, list_panes, get_pane_text, send_text
- WeztermClient: crates/wa-core/src/wezterm.rs — run_cli() at line 895, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS at line 388

## Expected Impact
- Eliminates stuck CLI processes entirely (0 subprocess spawning for pool-supported operations)
- Reduces latency from ~400ms (subprocess spawn) to ~1ms (socket reuse)
- Reduces CPU from process spawning overhead
- Reduces file descriptor usage from 250+/sec to 8 persistent connections
- Falls back to CLI for any operation not yet supported by DirectMuxClient

## Migration Path
1. Implement DirectMuxClientPool
2. Add pool to WeztermClient struct
3. Migrate list_panes() to pool (most common, test first)
4. Migrate get_text(), send_text() one at a time
5. Keep run_cli() as fallback for unmigrated operations

## Dependencies
None — this is foundational, other beads depend on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pool creates and manages DirectMuxClient connections
- Health checks detect and evict dead connections
- Concurrent operations use separate pool connections
- Idle connections evicted after timeout
- Fallback to CLI for unsupported operations
- No stuck processes under normal operation
- Metrics: pool size, acquire latency, health check failures

## Estimated Effort
4-6 hours implementation, 2 hours testing","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:17:31.915411Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:22:26.507668Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-41w","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:25.450214Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

- Eliminates stuck CLI processes entirely (0 subprocess spawning for pool-supported operations)

 {"id":"wa-4df","title":"[Human command] `wa backup export` (database + config archive with checksum)","description":"# Task: wa backup export

## Goal
Create portable backup archives that users can store, copy, and restore from.

## Command Design
```bash
# Basic export
$ wa backup export
Backup saved: ~/.local/share/wa/backups/wa_backup_2026-01-18_143015.wa

# Custom output
$ wa backup export --output /path/to/backup.wa

# With compression
$ wa backup export --compress

# Include specific workspaces only
$ wa backup export --workspace /project/a --workspace /project/b

# Exclude large transcript data (config/events only)
$ wa backup export --metadata-only
```

## Output Format
Archive structure (tar + optional zstd):
```
backup.wa/
├── manifest.json      # Version, timestamp, checksums
├── schema_version     # Plain text: "3"
├── database.sql       # SQLite dump
├── config.toml        # Effective configuration
├── patterns/          # Custom pattern packs
│   └── custom.toml
└── checksums.sha256   # Per-file checksums
```

manifest.json:
```json
{
  "wa_version": "0.1.0",
  "schema_version": 3,
  "created_at": "2026-01-18T14:30:15Z",
  "workspaces": ["/project/a", "/project/b"],
  "stats": {
    "segments": 12345,
    "events": 567,
    "panes": 8
  },
  "checksum": "sha256:abc123..."
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Use SQLite `.dump` for portability
- Acquire read lock during export (snapshot isolation)
- Stream large exports to avoid memory issues
- Verify checksum immediately after write

## Testing
- Unit tests: archive format validation, manifest generation
- Integration: export with active watcher, verify no corruption
- E2E: export + import round-trip preserves all data

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export creates valid, portable archive
- Checksum included and verified on write
- Progress shown for large databases
- Metadata-only mode for quick config backup

- Enforce max frequency + backoff

## Success Criteria

 {"id":"wa-4r7","title":"Multi-account exhaustion handling: all accounts at limit → graceful degradation","description":"# Multi-account exhaustion handling

## Purpose
Handle the scenario where ALL configured accounts have reached their usage limits simultaneously.

## Problem
Current usage limit workflow assumes at least one account is available for failover.
When all accounts are exhausted, we need graceful degradation:
1. Don't attempt impossible failovers
2. Provide clear status to user
3. Schedule auto-resume at earliest reset time
4. Support notification of this critical state

## Detection
```rust
// In account selection logic
let available = accounts.iter()
    .filter(|a| a.percent_remaining > 0.0 || a.reset_time < now)
    .collect::<Vec<_>>();

if available.is_empty() {
    return Err(AccountExhausted {
        next_reset: accounts.iter()
            .map(|a| a.reset_time)
            .min(),
        accounts_checked: accounts.len(),
    });
}
```

## Workflow Behavior
When `AccountExhausted` detected:

### Step 1: Capture state
- Save current session transcript
- Record in-progress task (if determinable)
- Log which accounts were checked

### Step 2: Calculate resume time
```rust
let earliest_reset = accounts.iter()
    .filter_map(|a| a.reset_time)
    .min();
```

### Step 3: Persist "awaiting reset" state
```sql
INSERT INTO workflow_paused 
(pane_id, reason, resume_at, state_snapshot)
VALUES (?, 'all_accounts_exhausted', ?, ?);
```

### Step 4: Notify user
```
🔴 All accounts exhausted

All 3 configured OpenAI accounts have reached their usage limits.

Next reset: account-2 @ 2026-01-19 00:00 UTC (6h 23m)

Options:
  1. Wait for reset (wa will auto-resume if enabled)
  2. Add new account: wa accounts add
  3. Continue manually in exhausted account (with limits)

Status: wa accounts status
Resume: wa workflow resume --when-available
```

### Step 5: Schedule resume (optional)
If auto-resume enabled:
- Create timer/cron for earliest reset time
- Resume workflow when triggered

## Configuration
```toml
[accounts.exhaustion]
notify_channels = ["desktop", "webhook"]
auto_resume_on_reset = true
save_transcript = true
```

## Robot Mode Error
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ALL_ACCOUNTS_EXHAUSTED",
    "message": "All configured accounts have reached usage limits",
    "data": {
      "accounts_checked": 3,
      "next_reset": "2026-01-19T00:00:00Z",
      "next_reset_account": "account-2"
    }
  }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit: Account selection returns exhaustion error correctly
- Unit: Resume time calculation
- Integration: Workflow pauses correctly
- E2E: Full exhaustion → notification → resume flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Exhaustion detected when all accounts at limit
- [ ] Clear user notification with reset times
- [ ] State preserved for resume
- [ ] Auto-resume works when enabled
- [ ] Robot mode returns structured error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:14.002558283Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:30:34.037935162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:30:34.037786926Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4r7","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4r7","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4r7","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4r7","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-c7o","title":"[EPIC] Phase 2: Workflow Engine & Automation","description":"# Phase 2: Workflow Engine & Automation

## Overview
This epic builds the workflow automation system that responds to detected patterns. The workflow engine is what makes wa "active" rather than just a passive monitor - it can take corrective actions when agents hit limits, experience compaction, need authentication, etc.

## Strategic Importance
The workflow system transforms wa from a "monitoring tool" into an "automation platform". This is where the real value emerges:
- Automatic context recovery after compaction
- Automatic account rotation on usage limits
- Automatic authentication renewal
- Foundation for more sophisticated multi-agent coordination

## Key Components

### 1. Workflow Engine Architecture
- Trait-based workflow definition: \`Workflow\` trait with name, description, handles, steps, execute_step
- Step results: Continue, Done, Retry, Abort, WaitFor
- WaitCondition: Pattern, PaneIdle, External
- Per-pane workflow locking (prevent conflicting workflows)
- Idempotent, recoverable, audited execution

### 2. Handle Usage Limits Workflow (Codex First)
Steps:
1. Exit current session gracefully (Ctrl-C twice for Codex)
2. Parse session info for resume (session ID, token usage)
3. Select next available account
4. Perform account authentication
5. Resume previous session
6. Send continue prompt

This is the most complex workflow and proves out the architecture.

### 3. Handle Compaction Workflow
Steps:
1. Wait for compaction to complete (stabilization window)
2. Send agent-specific context refresh prompt
   - Claude Code: "Reread AGENTS.md so it's still fresh in your mind."
   - Codex: "Please re-read AGENTS.md and any key project context files."
   - Gemini: "Please re-examine AGENTS.md and project context."

### 4. Browser Automation Skeleton
- Playwright integration structure
- Browser profile persistence
- OpenAI device auth flow (skeleton)
- Anthropic OAuth flow (skeleton)
- Google auth flow (skeleton)
- Semi-automated fallback (open browser for human when automation fails)

### 5. CAUT Integration
- Shell out to \`caut\` for usage tracking
- get_usage(service) - current usage for OpenAI/Anthropic/Google
- refresh_usage(service) - refresh from APIs
- Account selection logic based on percent_remaining

## Workflow Design Constraints
Every workflow must be:
- **Idempotent**: Re-running doesn't make a mess
- **Recoverable**: Can resume after a crash
- **Audited**: Every step recorded (what was observed, what was sent)
- **Guarded**: Each step validates state before action

## Workflow Execution Model
Each step follows: Observe → Decide → Act → Verify

Step can return:
- \`Continue\` - proceed to next step
- \`WaitFor(condition)\` - pause until condition met
- \`Retry(after)\` - retry same step after delay
- \`Abort(reason)\` - stop workflow with error
- \`Done(result)\` - workflow completed successfully

## Data-Driven Workflow Descriptors
In addition to compiled Rust workflows, support YAML/TOML descriptors for simple prompt injection sequences:

\`\`\`yaml
# ~/.config/wa/workflows/custom_compaction.yaml
name: custom_compaction_prompt
trigger:
  event: session.compaction
  agent: claude_code
steps:
  - wait_stable: 2s
  - send: |
      Please re-read AGENTS.md and the project's README.md.
  - wait_for: "❯"
\`\`\`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Workflow engine can execute multi-step workflows
- [ ] handle_compaction workflow works end-to-end for Claude Code
- [ ] handle_usage_limits workflow works end-to-end for Codex (at least skeleton)
- [ ] Browser automation structure is in place (can be extended in Phase 3)
- [ ] CAUT integration returns account usage data
- [ ] Workflows are audited and recoverable

## Dependencies
- Depends on Phase 1 (Foundation) - needs pattern engine, storage, watcher

## Technical Notes

### Why Codex First for Usage Limits?
- Codex has the clearest device auth flow
- Resume mechanism is well-documented
- Good test case for the complexity

### Semi-Automated Browser Auth
If Playwright can't complete auth (MFA, CAPTCHA, password):
1. Open non-headless browser
2. Request human to complete auth
3. Persist browser profile for future sessions
4. Notify when auth complete

This provides graceful degradation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:47:54.89521276Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:07.794108825Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:07.794108825Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of wa-nu4.1 (Phase 2: Workflows). wa-nu4.1 has correct dependency chain. Content merged conceptually."}
 {"id":"wa-cjn","title":"E2E test scripts with detailed logging","description":"## Scripts to Create

### scripts/e2e_test.sh (Master)
Runs all E2E tests, aggregates results

### scripts/test_wezterm_interface.sh
- Test list_panes with running WezTerm
- Test get_text retrieval
- Test send_text delivery
- Verify error handling

### scripts/test_storage.sh
- Create temp DB
- Insert segments
- Verify FTS search
- Test gap recording

### scripts/test_ingest.sh
- Start watcher against mock pane
- Verify segment capture
- Test gap detection

### scripts/test_patterns.sh
- Run pattern engine against corpus
- Verify all detections match expected

## Logging Requirements
All scripts must:
1. Print "[TIMESTAMP] [TEST_NAME] Starting..."
2. Print "[TIMESTAMP] [TEST_NAME] PASS/FAIL: reason"
3. Save full logs to /tmp/wa_e2e_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log
4. Support --verbose for extra output
5. Exit 0 if all pass, 1+ if any fail

## Acceptance
- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` runs without error
- Clear pass/fail output

 {"id":"wa-co0h","title":"FTUI-01.4 Risk register + rollback strategy for phased migration","description":"## Background\nMigration touches terminal ownership and operator UX; we need explicit fallback paths.\n\n## Deliverables\n- risk register (technical, operational, rollout)\n- trigger thresholds for rollback/canary pause\n- fallback procedures for feature-flag rollback\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- each high-risk area has owner, trigger, and mitigation\n- rollout tasks reference this register.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:36.833845531Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:52:08.830891011Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:52:08.830753756Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-co0h","depends_on_id":"wa-p85q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:36.846709263Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-co0h","depends_on_id":"wa-136q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:40.97829561Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
-{"id":"wa-cuz","title":"Design MuxSnapshot schema for full mux state serialization","description":"## Goal
Design and implement the core data structures that represent a complete, serializable snapshot of the WezTerm mux server state. This is the foundational data model for the entire session persistence feature.

## Background & Motivation
When the wezterm-mux-server process dies or needs to be restarted (e.g., due to memory leaks, protocol corruption after extended runtime, or upgrades), ALL running processes in all panes are killed because the mux server owns the PTY file descriptors. The kernel sends SIGHUP to all child processes. There is currently no way to save and restore session state across restarts.

WezTerm's Lua API provides building blocks (get_lines_as_escapes, inject_output, process/directory introspection, mux hierarchy enumeration) but no native serialization. The resurrect.wezterm plugin does this via Lua but is approximate and limited.

wa (wezterm_automata) already has: SQLite storage with WAL mode, pane tracking (PaneRecord), delta extraction, agent session correlation (AgentSessionRecord), and a vendored mux client. We can leverage all of this.

## Design Requirements
1. Define MuxSnapshot struct containing:
   - snapshot_id: String (UUID)
   - created_at: i64 (epoch ms)
   - mux_server_pid: Option<u32>
   - wezterm_version: String
   - wa_version: String
   - windows: Vec<WindowSnapshot>
   - workspaces: Vec<WorkspaceSnapshot>
   - active_workspace: Option<String>

2. Define WindowSnapshot containing:
   - window_id: u64
   - workspace: String
   - title: Option<String>
   - tabs: Vec<TabSnapshot>
   - active_tab_index: usize

3. Define TabSnapshot containing:
   - tab_id: u64
   - title: Option<String>
   - pane_tree: PaneNode (recursive tree for splits)
   - active_pane_id: u64

4. Define PaneNode enum:
   - Leaf(PaneSnapshot) for single panes
   - VSplit { left: Box<PaneNode>, right: Box<PaneNode>, ratio: f64 } for vertical splits
   - HSplit { top: Box<PaneNode>, bottom: Box<PaneNode>, ratio: f64 } for horizontal splits

5. Define PaneSnapshot containing:
   - pane_id: u64
   - domain: String
   - title: Option<String>
   - cwd: Option<String> (working directory)
   - foreground_process: Option<ProcessInfo>
   - dimensions: PaneDimensions (rows, cols, scrollback_rows)
   - cursor_position: Option<CursorPosition>
   - is_alt_screen: bool
   - user_vars: HashMap<String, String>
   - scrollback_hash: Option<String> (hash of scrollback content, for dedup)
   - agent_session: Option<AgentSessionRef> (link to wa's agent session tracking)

6. Define ProcessInfo:
   - name: String
   - argv: Option<Vec<String>>
   - pid: Option<u32>
   - is_whitelisted_for_restore: bool

7. Define AgentSessionRef:
   - agent_type: String (claude_code, codex, gemini)
   - session_id: Option<String>
   - wa_agent_session_id: Option<i64>

8. All structs must derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug
9. All structs must be roundtrip-safe through JSON and potentially TOON format
10. Include schema version field for forward compatibility

## Implementation Location
New file: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot_schema.rs
Re-export from crates/wa-core/src/lib.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- All structs compile and pass serde roundtrip tests
- Schema can represent arbitrary pane split topologies via recursive PaneNode
- JSON serialization produces human-readable output
- Schema version is included for migration support
- Integration with existing PaneRecord and AgentSessionRecord types

## Property Testing Requirements

### Proptest
Add `tests/proptest_snapshot_schema.rs`:
- **schema_versioning_compatibility**: For any MuxSnapshot with schema_version N (proptest generates versions 1..=current), assert that a deserializer for version current can read it (forward compatibility). For any MuxSnapshot with schema_version current, assert that fields not present in version N-1 are handled gracefully with defaults (backward compatibility). This ensures that snapshots created by older wa versions can always be read by newer wa versions.
- **pane_node_roundtrip**: For any arbitrary PaneNode tree (proptest generates random trees with 1-50 leaves), assert JSON roundtrip produces identical trees.
- **snapshot_completeness**: For any arbitrary MuxSnapshot (proptest generates), assert that every pane_id referenced in the PaneNode trees appears exactly once (no orphans, no duplicates).
- **optional_fields_resilience**: For any MuxSnapshot, randomly set optional fields to None (proptest generates a bitmask), serialize to JSON, deserialize, and assert all non-optional fields survive and optional fields are correctly None.

## Cross-References
- **wa-29k1** (SnapshotEngine orchestrator): wa-29k1 is the primary consumer of the MuxSnapshot schema. It populates the structs defined here during the capture pipeline. Any schema changes must be coordinated with the SnapshotEngine's capture logic.
- **bd-nz6** (SQLite tables for snapshot storage): wa-nz6 stores MuxSnapshot as JSON in the snapshot_json column. The schema must remain JSON-serializable, and any schema version bumps must be accompanied by migration logic in bd-nz6's storage layer. The snapshot_size_bytes column in wa-nz6 depends on the serialized size of the structs defined here.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:13:31.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:49:04.53138Z"}

8. All structs must derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug

## Acceptance Criteria

- Schema can represent arbitrary pane split topologies via recursive PaneNode
- JSON serialization produces human-readable output
- Schema version is included for migration support
- Integration with existing PaneRecord and AgentSessionRecord types

## Property Testing Requirements

### Proptest
Add `tests/proptest_snapshot_schema.rs`:
- **schema_versioning_compatibility**: For any MuxSnapshot with schema_version N (proptest generates versions 1..=current), assert that a deserializer for version current can read it (forward compatibility). For any MuxSnapshot with schema_version current, assert that fields not present in version N-1 are handled gracefully with defaults (backward compatibility). This ensures that snapshots created by older wa versions can always be read by newer wa versions.

The snapshot_size_bytes column in wa-nz6 depends on the serialized size of the structs defined here.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:13:31.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T22:21:55.482616-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:46:43.183861-05:00"}
 {"id":"wa-d0m4t","title":"Define Outcome<T,E> error handling strategy","description":"# Define Outcome<T,E> error handling strategy

## Background
Asupersync uses Outcome<T,E> instead of Result<T,E>. Outcome has four variants:
- Ok(T) — success
- Err(E) — application error
- Cancelled(CancelReason) — task was cancelled (user, timeout, fail-fast, race-loss, parent, shutdown)
- Panicked(PanicPayload) — task panicked

The severity lattice for composition: Ok < Err < Cancelled < Panicked (worst wins in joins).

## What needs to happen
1. Decide whether to adopt Outcome throughout the codebase or create adapter layers
2. If adapting: create From<Outcome<T,E>> for Result<T,E> and vice versa
3. If adopting: plan the gradual migration of return types
4. Handle the ? operator — Outcome implements Try trait differently than Result

## Recommendation
Start with adapter layer approach:
- Internal async code uses Outcome natively
- Public API boundaries convert to/from Result
- This minimizes blast radius while gaining cancel-correctness internally

## Key patterns to establish
- How to convert existing anyhow::Result → Outcome
- How to handle Cancelled at module boundaries
- How to propagate CancelReason through the call stack
- How to use cx.checkpoint()? for explicit cancellation points

## Acceptance criteria
- Error handling strategy document/module with conversion utilities
- Outcome adapter traits implemented
- Example migration of one simple async function showing the pattern

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest for Outcome composition laws**: Verify that Outcome's monadic operations obey algebraic laws:
  - `map`: identity law (`outcome.map(|x| x) == outcome`), composition law (`outcome.map(f).map(g) == outcome.map(|x| g(f(x)))`)
  - `and_then`: left identity, right identity, associativity
  - `or_else`: error recovery ordering, Cancelled vs Err priority respects severity lattice
  - Use `proptest!` with `prop::sample::Index` to generate arbitrary Outcome variants and verify laws hold across all variant combinations.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for Outcome vs Result overhead**: Measure that Outcome operations (map, and_then, or_else, ?) have zero-cost overhead compared to equivalent Result operations. Add `benches/outcome_vs_result.rs` comparing: construction, pattern matching, chaining 10 operations, and error propagation with ?. Target: identical codegen (verify with `--emit=asm` if needed).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:47:57.827179Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:46:48.314818Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-d0m4t","depends_on_id":"wa-hj458","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:48:17.400685Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-d248l","title":"Phase 3c: Rename feature flags vendored→frankenterm","description":"Rename feature flags in wa-core and wa Cargo.toml files:

## wa-core/Cargo.toml changes
- New: frankenterm = ["frankenterm-deps"]
- New: frankenterm-deps = ["dep:codec", "dep:config", "dep:mux", "dep:wezterm-term"]
- Changed: vendored = ["frankenterm"] (backward compat alias)
- Changed: vendored-wezterm = ["frankenterm-deps"] (backward compat alias)

## wa/Cargo.toml changes
- New: frankenterm = ["wa-core/frankenterm"]
- Changed: vendored = ["frankenterm"] (backward compat alias)

## Rationale
The name "vendored" no longer applies — we own the code. "frankenterm" signals the project's philosophy of combining best pieces from multiple sources. Backward compat aliases ensure existing CI/scripts/muscle-memory continue to work.

 {"id":"wa-dbxt","title":"FTUI-05.3 Migrate Panes view (filters, selection, bookmark signals)","description":"## Background\nPane targeting is core for safe automation and operator control.\n\n## Deliverables\n- pane list rendering and selection model\n- filter/bookmark/agent/domain indicators\n- parity checklist against existing behavior\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- pane operations remain discoverable and deterministic\n- filter interactions preserve previous semantics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:12.936330112Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:29:26.864964995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:29:26.864897309Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-dbxt","depends_on_id":"wa-38vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:12.95506327Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-dbxt","depends_on_id":"wa-1hbj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:58.707875333Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-dbxt","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:09.491818088Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
